stabilization_rule.definition: first checkpoint t such that joint_gap(t, final_checkpoint) <= epsilon and every later checkpoint also satisfies the same inequality
stabilization_rule.final_policy: same algorithm, same benchmark, same seed, final checkpoint

horizon_censoring.stable_before_horizon: stable_checkpoint is not null and stable_checkpoint < final_checkpoint
horizon_censoring.censored_at_horizon: stable_checkpoint is null or stable_checkpoint == final_checkpoint
horizon_censoring.interpretation: If censored_at_horizon is true, work_to_stable should be read as a training-budget ceiling, not as evidence of early sustained stabilization.

runtime_policy.reason: Runtime depends on hardware, BLAS/backend, cache effects, and implementation details; it is not used for cross-method speed conclusions.

amq.work_unit: sampled online TD target with one 2x2 minimax continuation game

fitted_minimax_dqn.native_step: neural fitting epoch after the model-based fixed point is computed
notes: Phase B is reported as secondary neural fitting work, not as additional Bellman work.
Routing AMQ checkpoints and DQN epochs are not directly comparable as raw steps.

amq.work_unit: sampled online TD target with one 2x2 minimax continuation game

fitted_minimax_dqn.work_unit: model target evaluation for one state-action pair
fitted_minimax_dqn.work_to_checkpoint: cumulative exact work counter stored in snapshot

amq.work_unit: sampled online TD target with one 2x3 minimax continuation game
amq.note: This is an extension benchmark; service-rate-control is not one of the original AMQ paper benchmarks.

fitted_minimax_dqn.work_to_checkpoint: cumulative exact work counter stored in snapshot

reporting_rules: Use work_to_stable as the main cross-algorithm speed metric.
Report censored_at_horizon counts whenever any method first satisfies the rule only at the final checkpoint.
Report both mean and median work ratios when seed-level stable work is heterogeneous.
Report stable_native_step as a secondary within-method learning-curve descriptor.
Plot joint_gap or policy_similarity against cumulative work on a log-scale x axis.
Do not use wall-clock runtime as main evidence.
Do not use defender-only gap or BVI-vs-DQN policy consistency for this convergence-speed module.
